title: Update Info page with expanded Hebrew studio content (RTL)

requirements.text: Replace the current studio description content on the Info page with the user-provided expanded Hebrew content, while keeping the existing page layout and styling conventions (same container/section structure and Tailwind class patterns) consistent with the rest of the site.

quotes:
Please update the content of the 'info' page. I want to replace the current text about the studio with the following expanded version. Make sure to keep the HTML structure (like

,

, or

classes) consistent with the rest of the site... Here is the new content to insert:

אולפן הקלטות והפקה מוזיקלית – deVee

ברוכים הבאים ל-deVee. יצרנו עבורכם אולפן הקלטות יוצא דופן...

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /info shows the new Hebrew heading and three expanded paragraphs (replacing the previous shorter text).
The overall layout remains consistent with the current Info page structure (Header, main container spacing, max width wrapper, section spacing, and typography classes remain in the same style).
No English studio-description section/content is introduced on the Info page.

requirements.text: Ensure the updated Info page renders Hebrew correctly with right-to-left (RTL) support, including correct text alignment, directionality, and bold styling consistent with the codebase’s existing HTML/JSX conventions.

quotes: ...ensure the Hebrew text is rendered correctly with RTL support.

acceptanceCriteria: The Hebrew content on /info is rendered RTL (e.g., the content container has RTL direction and right alignment).
Bold emphasis in the provided content is preserved and rendered correctly (using the project’s existing JSX/HTML conventions for emphasis).

There are no malformed/invalid tags introduced from the pasted HTML (e.g., no broken

tags or stray line breaks that break rendering).

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui.
Keep the app’s existing routing behavior (/info renders the Info page) unchanged.
nonGoals: Adding new pages, navigation items, or admin content editing features.
Design/theme refresh beyond keeping the existing site styling consistent.
